industry: Enterprise Software / IT Service Management (ITSM) & Customer Experience (CX)
thesis: Freshworks is a mid-cap enterprise SaaS vendor with a fast-growing Employee Experience (Freshservice) segment and a slower but stabilizing Customer Experience (Freshdesk/Freshdesk Omni) segment. Q1 2026 showed 16% revenue growth, 80.8% non-GAAP gross margin, and $55.4M in free cash flow (24% margin). With EX ARR above $540M growing 27% YoY, AI attach rates over 65% in new deals, and a restructuring plan targeting 20% annual adjusted FCF-per-share compounding, the stock trades at a discounted ~2.3x EV/Sales and ~9% FCF yield. The risk/reward is attractive if management stabilizes CX and executes the integration of FireHydrant.

drivers: CX ARR reaccelerates to high-single-digit growth while EX sustains 20%+ growth
AI attach rates and Freddy MCP Gateway drive net revenue retention above 110%
FireHydrant integration expands ITOM TAM and cross-sell penetration
FCF margins approach 30% and the stock re-rates to 4x EV/Sales

base_case.assumptions: Revenue grows 14–15% in FY2026, non-GAAP operating margins expand modestly, and adjusted FCF reaches ~$250M. The market awards a 2.5–3.0x EV/Sales multiple, reflecting a SaaS compounder with durable but decelerating growth and improving margins.

risks: CX ARR stalls or declines, dragging headline growth below the mid-teens
Agentic AI disrupts seat-based SaaS pricing and compresses blended ARPU
Competition from ServiceNow, Zendesk, HubSpot, and Salesforce accelerates churn
Restructuring or FireHydrant integration falters, delaying margin expansion

catalysts.event: Earnings report in early August 2026; key for restructuring impact and margin guidance
November 2026 earnings; watch for CX stabilization and EX retention trends
Full integration of FireHydrant ITOM capabilities into Freshservice expected through 2026
Potential formalization of $1B+ ARR / $1.3B+ revenue / $425M+ FCF 2028 outlook

key_risks: Slowing CX ARR growth, guided to low single digits in 2026, drags overall revenue
AI disruption of traditional seat-based SaaS pricing and pricing power
Intense competition from ServiceNow in ITSM and Zendesk, HubSpot, Salesforce in CX
Execution risk on the ~11% global headcount reduction and cost restructuring
Integration risk from FireHydrant and Device42 acquisitions
Macro enterprise spend pressure lengthening sales cycles and pressuring renewals
